description: Rules for generating macOS audio/music/MIDI apps with AudioKit. Captured from successful QAFlywheel runs against the audiokit-midi-synth scaffold.

guidelines.rule: AudioKit Package.swift MUST contain ALL THREE scaffold dependencies.
guidelines.why: The scaffold injected above has EXACTLY 3 .package() entries and 3 .product() entries. Your generated Package.swift MUST have the same 3 of each — do NOT drop any. The three packages provide DIFFERENT types, and the scaffold code references all of them. Type-to-package mapping: (a) AudioKit package → AudioEngine, Mixer, Settings, MIDINoteNumber, AmplitudeEnvelope. (b) AudioKitUI package → FFTView, NodeOutputView, and related visualization types. (c) SoundpipeAudioKit package → DynamicOscillator, Oscillator, CostelloReverb, Reverb, Delay, Distortion, FMOscillator, FMOscillatorBank, OscillatorBank, PluckedString, and most concrete DSP nodes. If your code uses ANY of those Soundpipe types (which the scaffold does), you MUST keep SoundpipeAudioKit in the dependencies — dropping it is the #1 reason AudioKit builds fail with 'cannot find type X in scope'. Versions: copy from: "5.6.0" / "0.3.0" / "5.6.0" byte-for-byte — DO NOT use 5.10.0 / 5.12.0 / 6.0.0 / ^5.0.0 (those don't exist). Platform: platforms: [.macOS(.v14)] — do NOT write .v12, .v13, .v15, or .v16. The scaffold code uses @Observable which requires macOS 14+.

guidelines.rule: Preserve the scaffold's exact Swift filenames and class names.
guidelines.why: The scaffold ships with specific filenames (SynthConductor.swift, ContentView.swift, AudioKitMIDISynthApp.swift) and class names (SynthConductor, SimplePianoKeyboard). Do NOT rename these to 'AudioEngineManager.swift', 'SynthEngine.swift', 'AudioManager.swift' etc. — the scaffold's tests and the auto-fix re-injection both reference the original names. Renaming breaks subsequent auto-fix cycles because the reinjected scaffold no longer matches your output's file structure. Extend by ADDING files (e.g., 'SequencerGridView.swift' for a sequencer add-on), not by renaming existing ones.
examples: Keep: Sources/AudioKitMIDISynth/SynthConductor.swift (class SynthConductor). Add: Sources/AudioKitMIDISynth/SequencerGridView.swift (new file for a sequencer grid).

guidelines.rule: AudioKit 5.x removed the `AK` class prefix — types are `Oscillator`, `Reverb`, `Table`, NOT `AKOscillator`, `AKReverb`, `AKTable`.
guidelines.why: AudioKit 5.0 (released 2020) dropped the `AK` prefix from every public type. Code like `AKOscillator`, `AKTable(.sawtooth)`, `AKReverb()`, `AKAmplitudeEnvelope`, `AKMixer` etc. will ALL fail with `cannot find 'AKXxx' in scope` against AudioKit 5.6+. The tutorials, Stack Overflow answers, and older docs that LLMs memorize are pre-5.0 and use the prefix — your fresh code MUST NOT. Strip the prefix from EVERY AudioKit type you reference: AKOscillator → Oscillator; AKTable → Table; AKReverb → Reverb; AKMixer → Mixer; AKAmplitudeEnvelope → AmplitudeEnvelope; AKEngine → AudioEngine (this one also renamed); AKSequencer → AppleSequencer; etc. Check each import site and each type reference. A single missed `AK` prefix breaks the whole file's type checking.

guidelines.rule: Use Conductor pattern, not raw engine in views.
guidelines.why: Wrap AudioEngine, oscillators, effects in an ObservableObject 'Conductor'. Views call high-level methods (noteOn, setReverbAmount). Never connect AudioKit nodes from inside View body.
examples: final class SynthConductor: ObservableObject { private let engine = AudioEngine(); ... }

guidelines.rule: AudioEngine.start() on main thread, after view appears.
guidelines.why: Call try engine.start() inside .onAppear, not in the conductor's init(). Calling from background thread or before the view is mounted causes silent failure on macOS 14+.

guidelines.rule: Microphone entitlement required even for output-only apps.
guidelines.why: AudioKit initializes the audio I/O subsystem, which requires com.apple.security.device.microphone entitlement. Without it, engine.start() throws on sandboxed apps. Use frameworks/_index/entitlements/audiokit.plist as the template.

guidelines.rule: Map MIDI velocity (0-127) to amplitude (0.0-1.0) explicitly.
guidelines.why: DynamicOscillator.amplitude expects 0.0–1.0. MIDI velocity is 0–127. Always divide: oscillator.amplitude = AUValue(velocity) / 127.0. Don't pass raw velocity.

guidelines.rule: AudioKitUI KeyboardView needs explicit frame height.
guidelines.why: Without a height constraint the keyboard collapses to 0pt. Wrap with .frame(height: 120-160).
AudioKit can't reconnect graph nodes while running. Connect oscillator → envelope → effects → mixer → engine.output before calling engine.start(). To swap nodes later, stop, reconnect, restart.
Don't slap @available(macOS 26.0, *) on AudioKit-using types — keep at 12.0. Reserve 26.0 only for app-level SwiftUI types using the latest APIs.

guidelines.rule: Package.swift target `path:` MUST point to the directory where Swift files actually live.
guidelines.why: If your target uses `path: "Sources/MyTarget"`, then ALL of the target's .swift files MUST be inside `Sources/MyTarget/`. Do NOT mix: do not put App.swift, ContentView.swift, etc. directly under Sources/ and ALSO declare `path: "Sources/MyTarget"` — Swift Package Manager will fail with `invalid custom path 'Sources/MyTarget' for target 'MyTarget'` because the path is empty or doesn't contain matching .swift files. Two valid layouts: (A) Convention: omit `path:` entirely AND put files under `Sources/{TargetName}/*.swift`. (B) Custom: include `path: "Sources/MyTarget"` AND put files under `Sources/MyTarget/*.swift`. Pick ONE — never mix the two for the same target. The scaffold uses layout B with `path: "Sources/AudioKitMIDISynth"` so every file must be under that subdirectory.

guidelines.rule: AudioKit 5.x removed the `>>>` connection operator — use explicit node init instead.
guidelines.why: AudioKit 5.x and later does NOT define the `>>>` operator. Code like `engine.output = osc >>> reverb` will FAIL with `cannot find operator '>>>' in scope`. To wire an effect onto a source, pass the source as the first argument to the effect's initializer: `let chain = Reverb(osc)`. To chain multiple effects: `let delay = Delay(reverb)`. To mix multiple sources: `let mixer = Mixer(osc1, osc2); let reverb = Reverb(mixer)`. Then `engine.output = chain`. The `>>>` operator existed in AudioKit 4.x and earlier and gets memorized by LLMs from old tutorials — it's gone now.

guidelines.rule: Reverb initializer takes the SOURCE node — not zero args.
guidelines.why: AudioKit effect nodes (Reverb, Delay, Distortion, Compressor, etc.) require a source node as their first init parameter. Calling `Reverb()` with no arguments will FAIL with `missing argument for parameter #1 in call`. Always wire the source: `Reverb(osc)`. The Apple `AVAudioUnitReverb` (different framework) is what takes no args — don't confuse the two.

_v1_migration_notes: Converted 2026-05-05 from v1 schema (rules/title/guidance/applies_when) to v2 (guidelines/rule/why/appliesTo). Original lastUpdated: 2026-04-15, scaffold: frameworks/macos/audiokit-midi-synth.
